products.title: NI 9381 C Series Multifunction I/O Module
products.body_html:
Overview
Manuals
SPECIFICATIONS
Need More Info
Features:
  • Compact Design
  • Reads Analog and Digital Signals
  • High Accuracy
The National Instruments NI 9381 C Series Multifunction I/O Module is a single, cost-effective module for general-purpose system I/O. The NI 9381 combines common I/O circuitry into a single module to help system designers fit more functionality into a single system. The analog circuitry on the NI 9381 is a multiplexed architecture that shares a single timing engine. The maximum sample rate per channel is divided by the number of both analog input and analog output channels in use.
The NI 9381 C Series Multifunction I/O Module may still be available for purchase and support from Artisan Technology Group beyond End-Of-Life (EOL) by the manufacturer (OEM).

  • Voltage / Current Limits:
     • Max voltage: 30 Vrms / 42 Vpk / 60 VDC
     • Max current: ≤ 1 A per channel 

  • Wire Size: Supports 16 to 26 AWG copper wire (with ~4.5 mm of insulation stripped)Operating Temperature: –40 °C to +70 °C

  • Screw Torque: Up to 0.4 N·m (≈ 3.4 lb·in) for the screw terminals 

  • Weight: ~65 g

products.title: NI-9981
products.body_html:
What is NI-9981

  • The NI-9981 is a backshell connector kit providing strain relief and operator protection for C Series modules that have a 10-position spring terminal connector. 

  • Essentially, it’s a protective housing (or cover) that goes over the spring terminal connection to reduce the risk of accidental contact and mechanical stress on the wires. 

Key Features & Details

Feature Description
Intended Use For 10-position spring terminal modules (C Series) 
Protection Prevents accidental touching of live contacts, improves safety 
Strain Relief Helps maintain cabling integrity by reducing stress on wires 
Part Variants Part numbers include 783787-01 and 783529-01
Compatibility Works with any module having a 10-position spring terminal interface

products.title: 1 Pcs NI 9923 Front Mounting Bolt Wiring Box for 37-Pin D-SUB Module 781503-01
products.body_html:
What is it?

  • NI-9923 (P/N 781503-01) is a front-mount screw terminal block / terminal adapter for 37-pin D-Sub modules. 

  • It provides a female 37-pin screw terminal interface on the front, which connects via a female 37-pin D-Sub connector on the back. 

  • It is intended to facilitate wiring of signals to/from C Series modules that use 37-pin D-Sub connectors, by converting the D-Sub to screw terminals for easier field wiring. 

Technical Specs & Usage

  • Compatible with NI C Series modules that have 37-pin D-Sub connectors.

  • The terminal block provides strain relief mounting holes and mechanical support for wiring. 

  • It’s rated for use up to 60 VDC / 30 Vrms (for typical applications) — this is typical for NI screw terminal blocks in similar series. The screw terminals are accessible after removal of captive screws

products.title: 194612-02 Test Cable Assembly, Ethernet Cable, cRIO RJ50 Cable, 10-Pin Plug to 10-Pin Plug,
products.body_html:
What It Is

  • NI 194612-02 is a Test Cable Assembly, specifically an RJ-50 to RJ-50 Ethernet / cRIO cable. Connector style: 10-pin modular plug to 10-pin modular plug (RJ50) (10p10c) Cable length: 2.0 metersIt is unshielded (i.e. no shielding) according to the specification listing on RKTSTORE

Use / Application

  • This cable is used in NI / cRIO systems (CompactRIO or related NI measurement/control systems) that use RJ50 connectors for I/O or communication. It is designed to carry signals between modules or from the cRIO to external systems using that 10-pin RJ50 interface.

  • Because it's labeled “Ethernet / cRIO RJ50 cable,” it may be used in contexts where RJ50 is used for digital / I/O communication rather than standard RJ45 Ethernet

Key Specifications & Features

Feature Value / Description
Front-end connector RJ-50 female https://www.ni.com+2Apex Waves+2
Back-end connector Screw-terminal block for wires https://www.ni.com+2Apex Waves+2
Routing Pin 1 → Terminal 1, 2 → 2, … up to pin 10 → terminal 10; shield (S) is also routed to a terminal marked “S” https://www.ni.com+2Apex Waves+2
Operating temperature –40 °C to +70 °C https://www.ni.com

Usage & Applications

  • You plug an RJ-50 male cable from a device (e.g. NI-9237, SCXI) into the adapter’s RJ-50 female receptacle.

  • Useful in test setups, labs, or any scenario where you want to avoid soldering or crimp connectors for prototyping or easy reconfiguration.

1. Oil Lamp Reset Service

2. EPB Electronic Park Brake Reset

It is essential to maintain the electronic braking system's safety and efficiency.

3. BAT battery service

Allows the scan tool to evaluate the battery charge state, monitor the close-circuit current, register the battery replacement, and activate the rest state of the vehicle.

4. DPF Diesel Particulate Filter Reset

It primarily works for Diesel Particulate Filter systems with DPF regeneration, DPF component replacement teach-in, and DPF teach-in after replacing the engine control unit.

5. SAS Steering Angel Sensor Reset

It helps clear the memories of the fault steering angle sensor and plays a vital role in calibration.

6. TPMS Tire Pressure Monitoring System Reset

It makes sense to display sensor IDs from the vehicle's ECU, input TPMS sensor replacement IDs, and test sensors.

7. IMMO Key Programming

The unique car essential programming and read pin code function is mainly used to disable the lost vehicle keys and program a new replacement key fob.

8. ABS Bleeding

ABS brake bleeding to get a firm brake pad after air exhaustion after repairing the ABS pump system.

9. Sunroof Reset

The sunroof needs to reset when the sunroof control unit has a communication problem or is changed.

10. Suspension Reset

Try resetting the electronic suspension air ride with the massage system, closing all doors, and koeo flip through the messages system to suspension reset or on off.

11. Gearbox (Transmission )Reset

Procedure Reset Transmission Adaptive Learning DIY. Start the car and drive. Once you perform this procedure, the Engine Control Unit (ECU) and Transmission Control Unit (TCU) will work together to learn how you go by monitoring your driving pattern. After you follow this programming method of the TCU / ECU, drive the car.

12. AFS Headlamp Reset

'AFS' means Adaptive Front lighting System (at night with the Bi-Xenon headlights). It swivels on either side; pressing that button means they stay pointing straight ahead and don't turn when you turn the steering wheel.

13. Injector Coding

Leaking and dead injectors can be missed even when an injector is disabled. Other problems with the ignition system and mechanical components also may not show an rpm loss when an injector is turned off.

14. Throttle Adaptation

The throttle body is a tube containing a pivoting flat valve (butterfly) that controls the amount of air entering an engine. In an electronic fuel-injected car, a throttle position sensor and airflow sensor communicate with the computer, supplying the fuel needed at the injectors. It must require re-learning the throttle sensor position after clearing or repair.

15. Gear Learning

The scan tool monitors specific camshaft position sensor signals to determine if all conditions are met to continue with the procedure for Engine running.

16. EGR Reset

Reduce nitrogen oxides (NOx) in the exhaust gas and improve fuel economy at partial load.

17. A/F Reset

This function is applied to set or learn Air/Fuel ratio parameters.

18. Adblue Reset(Diesel Engine Exhaust Gas Filter)

After the diesel exhaust treatment fluid (car urea) is replaced or filled, the urea reset operation is required.

19. Airbag Reset

This function resets the airbag data to clear the airbag collision fault indicator.

20. Coolant Bleeding

This function activates the electronic water pump before venting it into the cooling system.

21. AT Learning

This function can complete the gearbox self-learning to improve gear-shifting quality. Disassembling or repairing the gearbox (after some of the car battery is powered off) will lead to shifting delay or impact problems. In this case, this function must be done so that the gearbox can automatically compensate according to the driving conditions to achieve a more comfortable and better shift.

22. Language Change

This function changes the system language of the vehicle's central control panel.

23. Nox Sensor Reset

A NOx sensor detects the engine exhaust's nitrogen oxide (Nx) content. If the Nox fault is re-initialized and the Nox catalytic converter is replaced, resetting the catalytic converter learned value stored in the engine Ecu is necessary.

24. ODO Reset

a) ODO reset is to copy, write, or rewrite the value of kilometers in the chip of the odometer by using a car diagnostic computer and data cable so that the odometer shows the actual mileage.

b)Usually, when the mileage is not correct due to the damaged vehicle speed sensor or odometer failure, it

is necessary to do ODO reset after maintenance.

25. Stop/Start Reset

This function is used to open or close the automatic start-stop position via setting the hidden place in ECU(provided that the vehicle has a remote role and is supported by hardware).

26. Seat Calibration

 This function is applied to match the seats with the memory function that is replaced and repaired.

27. Transport Mode

To reduce power consumption, the following functions may be disabled, including limiting the vehicle speed, not waking up the door opening network, disabling the remote control key, etc. At this time, the transport mode needs to be deactivated to restore the vehicle to normal.

28. Windows Calibration

This feature is designed to perform door window matching to recover ECU's initial memory and recover the power window's automatic ascending and descending functions.

The non-slip grip design and anti-drop case significantly extend the lifespan of the MUCAR CS6 car scanner.

(Before purchasing, you must ask if the product is suitable for your vehicle)

MUCAR CS6 supports 6 common vehicle systems:

The number of systems for system-wide diagnostics is determined by the model. Some models may have forty or fifty systems, while others have only twenty or thirty. Common systems include: ECM/TCM/ABS/SRS/BCM/EPS/IC/TPMS/AC, etc. The MUCAR CS6 supports six common vehicle systems (for each system diagnostic function: reading fault codes, clearing codes, reading data streams, and accessing vehicle information): ECM = Engine System, ABS = Anti-lock Braking System, SRS = Airbag Restraint System, TCM = Transmission System, BCM = Chassis Control System, TPMS = Tire Pressure Monitoring System.

NOTES AND TIPS:

The principle of vehicle diagnostics:

- Read the fault code -- Find the part that needs repairing using the fault code and repair the car. - After completing the repair, use the diagnostic tool to clear the fault code.

If you clear the error code immediately before the repair, the following two situations are possible:

- Clear the fault code; the warning light will go out and no longer illuminate, indicating that it was a temporary fault code. - Clear the fault code; the warning light will go out and then soon come back on, meaning the car needs repair. After the repair, you can use the diagnostic tool to clear the fault code. (We recommend not clearing the codes before any repairs or services have been performed. The codes will reappear if the underlying fault causing the code is not resolved.)

If you have any questions while using our MUCAR CS6 scanner, please test the scanner using the following suggestion:

Please check if your vehicle supports OBD-II protocols and has a standard 16-pin data connector. Please check the vehicle wiring and fuses for short circuits. Please try connecting the scanner to a different vehicle to see if the communication error occurs again.

The number of systems for system-wide diagnostics is determined by the model. Some models may have forty or fifty systems, while others have only twenty or thirty. After the repair, you can use the diagnostic tool to clear the fault code. (We recommend not clearing the codes before any repairs or services have been performed. The codes will reappear if the underlying fault causing the code is not resolved.)

If you have any questions while using our MUCAR CS6 scanner, please test the scanner using the following suggestion:

Please check if your vehicle supports OBD-II protocols and has a standard 16-pin data connector. Please check the vehicle wiring and fuses for short circuits. Please try connecting the scanner to a different vehicle to see if the communication error occurs again.

Approximately 7 service reset functions:

Resetting the maintenance light = resetting the oil:

The car's service indicator light illuminates, indicating that the car requires servicing. After changing the engine oil or replacing electrical components that monitor engine oil life, the service indicator light must be reset.

EPB: Brake pad reset = Resetting the electronic parking brake (circumstances that need to be reset:

A) After replacing the brake pads and the brake pad wear indicator light comes on. B) The brake pad warning light illuminates. C) After the brake pad sensor circuit was short-circuited and repaired. D) After replacing the servo motor.

TPMS: Resetting tire pressure:

If the car's tire pressure warning light is on, you must reset the tire pressure using the tire pressure reset function after maintenance and turn off the warning light. If the tire pressure is too low or there is a leak, replace or install tire pressure monitoring devices, and replace any damaged tires and sensors. For cars with tire pressure monitoring systems, the tires should be replaced after the repair is completed. Press Reset.

SAS: Steering angle reset:

Resetting the steering angle involves finding the car's relative zero position in order to continue driving in a straight line. Using this position as a reference, the control unit can calculate the precise angle for left and right turns.

ETS: Gas mapping = Electronic throttle valve reset

The gas correspondence consists of using the car decoder to initialize the car's throttle actuators, so that the control unit's learning value returns to its initial state to more accurately control and adjust the action of the accelerator pedal (or idle engine) to adjust the air intake quantity.

DPF/FAP:

DPF regeneration adjustment is required in the following situations: A) Replace the exhaust back pressure sensor. B) Remove or replace the particulate trap. C) Remove or replace the fuel additive injector. D) Remove or replace the catalytic oxidizer. E) DPF regeneration failed. The warning light will illuminate and be adjusted after the repair. F) When the DPF regeneration control module is repaired or replaced.

ABS exhaust = ABS ventilation

First, if the ABS system contains air, it is necessary to bleed the brake system using the ABS bleed function to restore the ABS system's brake sensitivity. Second, when replacing the ABS control unit, ABS pump, master cylinder, brake cylinder, brake lines, or changing the brake fluid, it is necessary to use the ABS bleed function to exhaust the ABS system.

Specifically for engine diagnostics:

Read Codes: Read the existing fault code in the current control unit. (Application: Generally, the car's instrument cluster will have a fault indicator light illuminated, and the current fault code can be read.) Clear Codes: Clear the fault code in the current control unit. (Application: Perform this function once the car problem is resolved; the warning light will turn off. If the current fault cannot be resolved, perform this function when maintenance is required.) Live Data: Reads and displays the operating data and parameters of the car's ECU in real time. (Application: By monitoring these data streams in real time, maintenance technicians can gain an overview of the vehicle's overall performance and provide guidance for vehicle maintenance.) Image Stop: When the vehicle experiences a fault, a fault code is generated, and the Image Stop function can be used to display the vehicle data at the time the fault code is generated. (Application: The maintenance technician assesses the fault location based on the relevant data.) I/M Readiness: Indicates whether the vehicle's various emissions-related systems are functioning properly and ready for inspection and maintenance testing. O2 Sensor Test: The O2 sensor test function allows you to retrieve and display the O2 sensor monitor test results from the vehicle's on-board computer to show the last test performed. On-Board Monitor Test: This function can be used to read the results of the on-board diagnostics monitoring test for a specific component/system. EVAP Test: The EVAP test function allows you to initiate a leak test of the vehicle's EVAP system. This tool does not perform a leak test itself but sends a signal to the vehicle's on-board computer to start the test. The vehicle manufacturer determines the standards and methods for stopping the test once it has begun. Before using the system's test function, please refer to the vehicle's service manual to determine the procedure required to stop the test.
